front: What is the role of dendritic cells in initiating adaptive immunity?
back: Dendritic cells (DCs) capture, process, and present antigens to T cells. Maturation: upregulate MHC, costimulatory molecules (CD80/86), cytokines. Cross-presentation activates CD8+ T cells. DC subsets: cDC1 (cross-presentation), cDC2 (CD4+ T cell priming), pDC (IFN-alpha).
